pi_items.source: Code analysis: task.json description line 5, export_result.sh line 33 SQL query, verifier.py line 59 lookup
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 33 SQL query 'SELECT ID, NAME FROM USER_TYPE'; Floreant POS documentation confirms user types are managed in a USER_TYPE database table
Code analysis: verifier.py line 121 'passed = record_found and (score >= 55)'

privileged_info_summary: Floreant POS is a real, open-source restaurant point-of-sale system (version 1.4 build 1707b) downloadable from SourceForge. It uses Apache Derby as the embedded database with the database name 'posdb'. The USER_TYPE table exists in Floreant POS and stores user role definitions. The task requires creating a new user type named 'Shift Supervisor' in the USER_TYPE table. The verifier requires both database record existence and a minimum score of 55 for task success.
